creator: fuz@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Yuri Victorovich from comment #18)

OpenSSL has frequent security issues that are very likely to be exploited.  Bundled versions are very likely to be out of date and cannot be updated with emergency patches by us.

For this reason, the ports tree gives you an error when DEVELOPER mode is enabled and it detects a bundled OpenSSL.  Please take the security of our users seriously.

Unbundling OpenSSL is very easy: just set some build flags and the crate will use the system's OpenSSL over the bundled one, eliminating the risk.  In fact, the maintainer had already prepared a patch for this purpose and was in the process of testing it when you went ahead and committed the initial, defective patch.  Please don't do this.
